mnemonics: PROCESS: Parents Run, Children Execute, System Survives
ENVIRON: Everything Needed In Variables Runs Our Node
keyPoints: Every process has a unique process ID (PID) and a parent process ID (PPID).
A process inherits its environment from its parent process via the `environ` variable.
The `main` function receives argument count (`argc`), argument vector (`argv`), and environment (`envp`) parameters.
commonMistakes: Assuming the environment pointer `environ` is passed as a third parameter to `main`; it is a global variable instead.
Confusing the process's command-line arguments (in `argv`) with its environment variables.
Believing a child process's environment is a live link to the parent's; it is a copy made at the time of the `fork()` call.
